name: Indigenous Fire Management
description: Multi-generational human ecological intelligence encoding tens of thousands of years of landscape management knowledge. Controlled low-intensity burns create mosaic patterns that enhance biodiversity, regulate water cycles, manage game habitat, and prevent catastrophic wildfire through fuel-load reduction. Timing synchronizes with humidity, wind, and plant phenology. Oral tradition transmits observational knowledge across generations — the longest-running adaptive management system on Earth.

core_attributes.neurology: multi-generational ecological memory via oral tradition
core_attributes.pattern: mosaic burning, landscape-scale optimization, fire-as-tool intelligence
